context:
INTEL
=====
📌Weaxor is a spin-off of Mallox Ransomware targets Windows and Linux Environment (as of now)
💡The group had used Cobalt Strike is being pulled from a dedicated Chinese Hosted Server, which is uncommon
💡The group also uses Lumma Stealer during their infection cycle
📌AS37963: Commonly (ab)used for CobaltStrike Activities
💡Earliest sample is traced in October 2024
💡Deliverable malicious executables are mostly named as Microsoft Products or Processes like OneDrive.exe, svchost2.exe etc
💡Uses cracked version of Cobalt Strike for attacks
💡Added new IP to pull the powershell file for infection 
💡Using Mallox Server for 4 years : 193.143.1.139

PAYLOAD ANALYSIS
================
📌Cobalt Strike beacon is injected → svchost.exe
📌CS Watermark: 987654321
📌C2Server: 47.109.38.125 , 193.143.1.153
📌Sleep Time: 3 Seconds (Sleep interval between callback attempts)
📌Jitter: 40% Randomized delay added to SleepTime for evasion
📌Payload Download Size: 4.2 MB
📌Avoids RWX memory regions to reduce detection risk
📌13 KB: Minimum memory to allocate before injecting code
📌bStageCleanup: Removes stager after beacon setup
📌bCFGCaution: May indicate beacon operates without safeguards
📌KillDate set to 0, as beacon stays forever


TOR DLS: weaxorpemwzoxg5cdvvfd77p3qczkxqii37ww4foo2n4jcft3mytbpyd.onion
